title: [4] MCP Server Phase 3 — Mutating CRUD Tools (Confirm Param + Delete Dry-Run)
why_we_need_this: Phase 2 (task #3, completed) shipped 10 read-only MCP tools, validating the MCP-to-dashboard stdio/HTTP round trip with zero mutation risk (`project-context/routes/mcp-server/README.md` L4-L7). Per the owner-approved 5-phase roadmap, Phase 3 is the first phase that lets the MCP server mutate WireGuard peer state — add/edit/delete/enable/disable via the dashboard's already-implemented `/api/clients*` endpoints (spec 019). The owner resolved the previously-open confirmation-gate question on 2026-07-06: mutating tools are not all treated the same — edit/enable/disable get a lighter gate, delete (the one non-reversible mutation) gets a stronger one.
acceptance_criteria:
- Read `project-context/routes/mcp-server/README.md` in full (current STATUS, invariants, and the now-resolved confirmation-gate decision below) plus `mcp/docs/tool-surface.md` (Phase 1 deliverable) and the Phase 2 code under `mcp/` to understand the existing tool registration pattern before adding new tools. Cross-check `clients-connectivity/README.md` for the confirmed `/api/clients*` semantics (SQLite as live source of truth, admin_peer seed rule) via `search_project_context` — do not assume behavior not documented there.
- Implement `edit_client`, `enable_client`, `disable_client` as tools that require an explicit `confirm=true` boolean parameter. If `confirm` is missing or false, the tool MUST return an error/explanation without calling the dashboard — it must NOT perform the mutation. No token, no second call — a single call with `confirm=true` executes immediately.
- Implement peer deletion as a two-tool, token-gated dry-run flow instead of a single `delete_client(confirm=true)`:
  - `preview_delete_client(name)` — calls a read-only dashboard endpoint (e.g. `GET /api/clients/{name}/config`) to fetch the peer's current state, generates a short-lived confirmation token bound to that specific peer name, and returns a human-readable preview (peer name, public key, enabled state, last-seen) plus the token. This call MUST NOT call `DELETE /api/clients/{name}` or mutate any state.
  - `delete_client(name, token)` — calls `DELETE /api/clients/{name}` only if `token` matches the token most recently issued by `preview_delete_client` for that exact `name` AND the token has not expired. Define and document a concrete expiry window (e.g. 5 minutes) and where the token is held (in-process memory of the running MCP server — no persistence to disk required, since the process is short-lived/stdio-spawned per the route's transport model). Wrong/expired/missing token → error, no deletion.
- Implement `add_client` per Phase 1's tool-surface doc — classify it as read-only-effect-adjacent (creates rather than destroys) and apply the same `confirm=true` pattern as edit/enable/disable (not the delete token flow), since Phase 1/owner discussion treated deletion as the sole irreversible-therefore-hardened case.
- All new tools call the dashboard's existing `/api/clients*` HTTP endpoints only — never touch SQLite or `wg` directly (wrapper-only invariant, `project-context/routes/mcp-server/README.md` L51).
- Reuse the existing HTTP client / target-address configuration (`172.16.15.1:8080` default, env/CLI override) already established in Phase 2 — do not introduce a second config mechanism.
- No Docker, no always-on HTTP/SSE listener — stdio subprocess transport only, consistent with Phases 1-2.
- Document the token-based dry-run mechanism (expiry window, in-memory storage, per-peer binding) in a short note under `mcp/docs/` (e.g. append to `tool-surface.md` or a new `mcp/docs/confirmation-gates.md`) so Phase 4 validation and future readers know the exact mechanics without reading the code.
- Do not add any application-layer auth beyond what's described above — the no-auth-at-the-dashboard-boundary model remains a settled, owner-accepted risk; this task's confirmation gates are a client-side safety mechanism, not an auth layer.
important_constraints:
- The confirmation-gate design is now RESOLVED per owner decision (2026-07-06): `confirm=true` param for edit/enable/disable/add; separate token-gated dry-run (`preview_delete_client` → `delete_client`) for delete only. Do not redesign or re-litigate this split.
- Live validation against the real production tunnel/dashboard is explicitly Phase 4 — do not claim this task's tools have been validated against production; unit/local testing against a reachable dashboard instance is in scope, production tunnel validation is not.
- Do not modify `dashboard/` or `terraform/` — this task only adds/extends files under `mcp/`.
- Pin any new Go module dependency versions exactly in `go.mod` — no `~>`/range-style versions.
- Do not edit anything under `project-context/` directly — that's Praxis-managed via `update_project_context`, out of scope for this task.
- Follow this repo's git conventions once code is committed: PR-based workflow, `feat:` commit prefix, `feat/`-prefixed branch name.
- After this task's work lands, run `update_project_context` (per the standard MCP lifecycle) so the mcp-server route reflects that Phase 3 code now exists and that the confirmation-gate question is resolved (not still "open").
